_comment: Eval corpus for skill-search. Each entry: a natural-language query + the slugs we expect
to appear in the top-K results. Used to regression-test embedding quality.
IMPORTANT: these tests are SKIPPED while the index uses the `mock` embedding provider —
mock embeddings are deterministic but not semantically meaningful, so any expected_top_k
assertion would always fail. The corpus exists as a regression harness for when the repo
owner enables a real provider post-seal.
3. (extend test-skill-discovery.mjs to read this file and assert expected_top_k subset)

tests.query: track new GitHub stars on watched repos
monitor DeFi protocols and token movements
summarize trending Hacker News stories
write a daily article about a trending topic
review pull requests on watched repos
audit skills for security vulnerabilities
track Polymarket prediction market price moves
compose a tweet from recent skill output
fix a failing skill automatically
publish an article to dev.to
produce a morning brief
research recent AI agent frameworks
track token unlocks and vesting schedules
manage fleet of forked Aeon instances
tally weekly cost of Anthropic tokens
Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday Show HN dispatch
scan trending GitHub repos and pick papers worth reading
weekly recap of what I shipped
spawn a new Aeon instance for a specific purpose
regenerate the public dashboard gallery from articles
